# Git Blame: Life Edition

## What Is This?

A humorous single-page web app that parodies `git blame` by blaming life problems on absurd culprits (your ex, Mercury retrograde, capitalism, etc.). Built with React, TypeScript, Vite, and Tailwind CSS. Fully client-side, no backend.

## Tech Stack

- React 18 + TypeScript (strict mode)
- Vite 5 (build tool)
- Tailwind CSS 3 (styling)
- ESLint 9 + TypeScript ESLint (linting)

## Architecture

Single-page app with phase-based state (`idle` → `blaming` → `done`). All state lives in `App.tsx`. No routing, no external state management, no API calls.

### Key Files

- `src/App.tsx` — Main component, orchestrates animation phases
- `src/data/blame.ts` — All blame data: culprits, blame lines, problems, terminal logs
- `src/utils/glitch.ts` — Text glitch effect and severity color helpers
- `src/components/` — Header, BlameInput, TerminalLog, BlameReport, BlameSummary, CRTOverlay
- `tailwind.config.js` — Custom theme with blame colors and CRT animations

## How to Run

```
npm install
npm run dev
```

## How to Build

```
npm run build
```

Output goes to `dist/`. Deploy to any static hosting.
